causal_question: Among districts with elections decided around the 50% Democratic vote-share threshold, what is the effect of electing a Democrat (versus a Republican) on the representative's likelihood of signing civil-rights discharge petitions in the 73rd Congress?
vague_question: In very close elections, does a Democrat barely winning change how likely the representative signs pro-civil-rights petitions in the 73rd Congress? By how much does this likelihood change?
